result.summary: monday.com exposes a GraphQL API, a Platform MCP server, and a CLI; auth uses bearer API tokens or OAuth for GraphQL, OAuth for MCP, and CLI auth remains undocumented.

monday_oauth_app.label: monday.com OAuth app
monday_oauth_app.setup: Create an OAuth app in monday.com’s developer portal, then use the registered client to start the OAuth flow. The auth and token endpoints are `https://auth.monday.com/oauth2/authorize` and `https://auth.monday.com/oauth_ms/oauth/token`. Request the least-privilege scopes your app needs; monday documents scopes such as `me:read`, `boards:read`, `boards:write`, `docs:read`, `docs:write`, `workspaces:read`, `workspaces:write`, `users:read`, `users:write`, `account:read`, `notifications:write`, `updates:read`, `updates:write`, `assets:read`, `tags:read`, `teams:read`, `teams:write`, `departments:read`, `departments:write`, `webhooks:write`, `webhooks:read`, and `ai:consume`.

monday_personal_api_token.label: monday.com personal API token
monday_personal_api_token.setup: Sign in to monday.com, open your profile/developer settings, and create a personal API token. Use it as a bearer token in the `Authorization` header for the monday GraphQL API.

monday_mcp_oauth.label: monday.com MCP OAuth access
monday_mcp_oauth.setup: Use the monday MCP connect flow described by monday’s MCP documentation. Authenticate with your monday.com account and grant the app the scopes it requests; the MCP server uses OAuth derived from the server’s well-known metadata.
